programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present an edition of the quiz featuring people famed for their voices, with contestants India Fisher, Nicholas Briggs, Kate Robbins, Steve Nallon, Redd Pepper, Richard Ridings, Felicity Finch and Tim Bentinck(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Joe Crowley is in Cumbria to explore the Lake District's wild side. meeting a man who runs pony treks that follow old pack-horse routes across the fells. Joe then hears more about the region's industrial heritage as he meets artists behind a new installation celebrating the area's copper mining history. Plus, Charlotte Smith investigating the rise of far-right extremism in the countryside(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from the first two live weekends. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off to decide who will be the first to leave the contest. With music by Lewis Capaldi(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Lytham Hall in Lancashire, where items brought in include a rare Victorian automaton, a work by LS Lowry and medals won by Britain's first black Olympian. John Foster marvels at a briefcase full of artefacts from Ernest Shackleton's 1909 Antarctic expedition and Ronnie Morgan discovers a gift from a Russian tsar(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: Drama following people from all walks of life during the first year of the Second World War, beginning in the run-up to its outbreak. American journalist and broadcaster Nancy Campbell makes a grisly discovery at the Polish-German border, while in Manchester, Lois waits for news of her translator boyfriend Harry. However, Lois is unaware that Harry has a secret Polish lover, Kasia, and he has vowed to help her flee from Warsaw. Starring Sean Bean, Helen Hunt, Lesley Manville, Julia Brown and Jonah Hauer-King(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Leicester City v Newcastle United. Mark Chapman presents action from the only Premier League fixture of the day, held at King Power Stadium. The sides have already met in the second round of the Carabao Cup this term, with Leicester prevailing on penalties at St James' Park after the match ended 1-1. Plus, a round up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Kelly Somers presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal Women v Brighton & Hove Albion Women at Meadow Lane, Manchester United Women v Liverpool Ladies at Leigh Sports Village, and West Ham United Ladies v Tottenham Hotspur Women at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: A World Athletics Championships edition of the quiz, which sees former Great Britain stars Denise Lewis, Liz McColgan, Greg Rutherford and Kriss Akabusi join host Sue Barker and team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson on the quiz(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets Oscar-winning actress Helen Mirren, who is starring in the new TV drama Catherine the Great. Author, adventurer and documentary-maker Simon Reeve also appears, as does comedian and actor Jack Whitehall, who stood in as host for Graham during the spring. Plus, the one and only Rupaul discusses bringing his hit show to the BBC in RuPaul's Drag Race UK. Music is supplied courtesy of Danish popsters Alphabeat(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Consumer affairs show tackling issues around the UK. In a battle against suspect deals, the team digs into the facts and demands answers from people at the top, as well as showing viewers how to get the necessary information to make practical choices about their finances(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin explore a two-bed terrace in Salford, a three-bed semi in Derbyshire and a one-bedroom flat in south London(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: Following the investigation by an elite team of Scotland Yard detectives into the biggest organised crime gangs in London, which uncovered the drugs kingpin behind a multimillion-pound racket. By making his own drugs delivery, Paul Monk blew his cover and, when his associates were arrested, fled abroad(n)

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Rob and Gerald brave the red carpet at a celebrity hotspot in London to try to recover the unpaid wages of a former worker, and there is an angry confrontation in a motorcycle shop when Mike and Lawrence try to remove bikes to collect a debt owed to a customer(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and Nick Hall help two teams look for items in the West Sussex town of Ardingly, while presenter Anita Manning investigates the history of funfairs(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Eve is a little more optimistic about the future and Valerie faces a tragedy, while Rob sustains an injury at work, and one of his colleagues acts strangely around the hospital(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in rural Somerset with two sport enthusiasts who have a £1million budget and want to buy a country home with views and a garden for football(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell go in search for items in Cumbria, setting out from Cockermouth and heading towards an auction near Kendal(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal is joined by three of the couples, including the first to be eliminated from the competition after losing the dance-off(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Matt Baker and Michelle Ackerley present(n)

programme.title: Inside Out South
programme.desc: A report on a plan to save the seaweed forests off the Sussex coast that has the backing of David Attenborough and which could help combat climate change. Plus, the programme goes on the trail of a rogue travel agent who took thousands of pounds from families to pay for holidays that were never booked, leaving them to pay twice or go without(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben agrees to go on a date with Callum, although he begins to wonder if it is such a good idea after both Ian and Jay comment on his history with men - and things only get worse when Stuart pleads with him not to hurt his brother. Bex is fed up of Sonia fussing over university so by way of distraction she organises a baby shower for Louise. Mel continues to pressurise Sharon into getting her a villa in Portugal, Denise confronts Wanda over her treatment of Ted and Jean supports Daniel at his hospital appointments(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A year on from the assassination of Jamal Khashoggi, Jane Corbin investigates the brutal murder of the journalist inside the Saudi consulate in Istanbul. She speaks to some of the very few people who have listened to covert recordings from inside the diplomatic building, revealing the full details of what happened to Khashoggi, the hit squad sent to kill him and the cover-up that followed. She also hears evidence that responsibility for the murder goes to the highest levels of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and asks whether the West has turned a blind eye to state-sanctioned murder so that lucrative trade deals can flourish(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The eight remaining contestants take part in the first of the semi-finals and face their toughest challenge yet as they battle gale force winds as they cook in tents perched high on top of the White Cliffs of Dover. They need to defy the elements to serve up a feast for 120 HM Coastguard staff and volunteers responsible for keeping one of the world's busiest shipping lanes safe. They then head back to the MasterChef kitchen for an invention test, before two more celebrities are sent home(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a documentary following young people from two local estates in London's Waltham Forest as they take part in a ground-breaking arts intervention programme designed to change the course of their lives for good. With help from mentors including Young Vic artistic director Kwame Kwei-Armah, EastEnders star Tameka Empson and composer Talvin Singh, the cast write and perform a musical based on their lives, as part of the capital's first Borough of Culture celebrations(n)

programme.title: The Cameron Years
programme.desc: The second of the two-part documentary begins in 2005 at a moment of crisis for the Conservative Party, following three successive general election defeats. David Cameron, a relatively new MP, and his small group of supporters sought to radically shift the party's direction, taking on veteran opponent David Davis in a leadership election. Here, Cameron re-examines his desire to reshape the party - and how he risked alienating the powerful old guard as he set out to prioritise issues such as environmentalism, social justice and international aid(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Ali-A's Superchargers
programme.desc: A car gets a new look inspired by planes(n)

programme.title: Top Class
programme.desc: Two teams of CBBC stars battle it out(n)

programme.title: Mustangs FC
programme.desc: Marnie is forced to play friendship umpire(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Radio 1's Maya Jama is in charge as Guest Editor and we launch a brand new Blue Peter badge. Find out if Lindsey has got what it takes to conduct a world class orchestra, hear Paul McCartney's musical memories, and there's a festival in the studio with special musical guests(n)

programme.title: Super Senses: The Secret Power of Animals
programme.desc: The concluding episode explores the world of scent and examines the animals that have pushed their sense of smell far beyond human capabilities. In the Bahamas, physicist Helen Czerski dives into shark-infested waters with only a small pouch of liquid as her defence against them, while biologist Patrick Aryee controls the behaviour of a swarm of bees by using tiny traces of scent, and gets uncomfortably close to a skunk(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: The third leg of Dave Myers and Si King's trip sees them on the trail of Oklahoma's famous beef. In Oklahoma City, a classic T-bone steak is followed by a visit to the city's Vietnamese community, where they knock up their national dish pho - a broth made of beef or chicken. After checking out El Reno's unique onion burger the duo hook up with a couple of fire crews who like to cook - especially brisket. An organic sweet potato farm inspires Dave and Si to make sweet potato brownies, before they finish this stretch on a cattle ranch with America's most iconic custodian of the land - the cowboy(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara Ó Briain and Angela Scanlon present the grand final, which features the biggest ever Robot Wars battle. One of the six places in the last round remains free, and to decide who gets it, all the contestants who came second or third in the past five episodes return to battle to the death. The winner in this Ten Robot Rumble will have to face the five episode winners through a series of group battles and knockout rounds before the final showdown to decide which machine will be crowned series champion(n)

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Wildlife documentary making use of scientific research into animal anatomy to explain how various species are uniquely built to thrive in specific habitats. Chris Packham begins by examine details the penguin, detailing how the aquatic, flightless bird's legs, wings and body shape have allowed it to conquer a wide range of landscapes, from deep forests and tropical waters to bustling cities and the toughest place on the planet - Antarctica(n)

programme.title: Live Cycling
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents live coverage of the women's race, as the riders negotiate a hilly 149km course that starts in Bradford and finished in Harrogate, taking in the Yorkshire Dales en route. Great Britain's Lizzie Deignan is aiming to emulate her 2015 triumph in Richmond, USA, having returned to the sport following the birth of her first child, but she is expected to face stiff competition from reigning champion Anna van der Breggen and her Dutch compatriot Annemiek van Vleuten. With commentary by Simon Brotherton, Chris Boardman and Rochelle Gilmore(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents further coverage of day two of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar, including the men's 100m final. Justin Gatlin is the defending champion, having produced an upset in Usain Bolt's last-ever race, with the Jamaican only managing bronze, as Gatlin's American compatriot Christian Coleman took silver. Medals are also decided in the women's hammer and 10,000m and the men's long jump, and British medal hope Lynsey Sharp is expected to be involved in the semi-finals of the women's 800m. With analysis from Michael Johnson(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: The older members of the platoon are threatened with being made air-raid wardens, so they take drastic action in a bid to appear younger - with Mainwaring even wearing a toupee. Comedy first aired in 1972, starring Arthur Lowe, John Le Mesurier and Clive Dunn(n)

programme.title: Marianne and Leonard: Words of Love (2019)
programme.desc: Documentary film-maker Nick Broomfield charts the relationship between Leonard Cohen and his Norwegian muse Marianne Ihlen, who met on the Greek island of Hydra in 1960 as part of a bohemian community of artists, writers and musicians. The film follows their relationship from the early days on Hydra, a humble time of 'free love' and open marriage, and reveals how their love evolved when Leonard became a successful musician(n)

programme.title: Annie Hall (1977)
programme.desc: A neurotic and self-obsessed New York Jewish comedian looks back on his affair with a scatterbrained singer, from their first encounter to the seemingly inevitable breakdown of communication, and tries to work out what went wrong. Oscar-winning romantic comedy, directed by and starring Woody Allen, with Diane Keaton, Shelley Duvall, Paul Simon, Christopher Walken and Carol Kane(n)

programme.title: Night Train to Lisbon (2013)
programme.desc: An academic impulsively pursues a woman he met by chance, leading to him abandoning his job to follow her to Lisbon. On the journey, he reads a book belonging to her, and is inspired by the philosopher who wrote it. He sets out to track down everyone who knew the author to discover the story of his life. Drama based on Pascal Mercier's novel, starring Jeremy Irons and Melanie Laurent(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game. Plus, a look at the major news stories shaping the American football world, as the leading sides in the NFL continue their bids to succeed Philadelphia Eagles as Super Bowl-winners(n)

programme.title: Life in a Cottage Garden with Carol Klein
programme.desc: In May and June, the horticulturist plants sweet peas, picks the year's first salad leaves and stakes perennials into the ground. She also witnesses the blossoming of bluebells, primal ferns and oriental poppies, before she and her husband Neil take delivery of their first hive of honey bees(n)

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks back on her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture, picking out her favourite tips from fellow presenters. The series progressed in order from A to Z, featuring gardening themes beginning with all 26 letters(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: A look at the role that plants and gardening play in people's mental and physical wellbeing, exploring the idea that gardening is a good and positive pastime. Arit Anderson travels to a medical centre in Kent where gardening is prescribed by doctors, to help patients cope with long-term mental and physical problems. Adam Frost is at a community garden in Oxfordshire, created to provide ongoing support for sufferers of Parkinson's disease. Back at Longmeadow, Monty Don plants bulbs for spring and catches up on some seasonal tasks(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The programme plays host to One Man and His Dog 2019, as the best shepherds from across the British Isles descend on the Scottish Borders to find out which nation has what it takes to claim this coveted title. Matt Baker and Helen Skelton present proceedings from Bowhill House, as the best youngsters and senior shepherds from England, Wales, Ireland and Scotland are teamed up in a bid to win the trophy for their country(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Live Cycling
programme.desc: Live coverage of the men's race, as the riders negotiate a 285km course that starts in Leeds and finishes in Harrogate. Spain's Alejandro Valverde prevailed in this race last year, out-sprinting Romain Bardet of France and Canada's Michael Woods, who claimed silver and bronze respectively. With commentary by Simon Brotherton, Chris Boardman and Rochelle Gilmore(n)

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Salford Red Devils v Castleford Tigers and St Helens v Wigan Warriors. Action from the semi-finals, held at AJ Bell Stadium and Totally Wicked Stadium respectively. With commentary by Matt Newsum(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of day three of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar, including the women's 100m final. Great Britain's Dina Asher-Smith has been in excellent form this year and will hope to be in the mix for medals in the sprint event, but could face stiff competition from Jamaican Olympic champions Shelly-Ann Fraser-Pryce and Elaine Thompson. Elsewhere, medals are decided in the men's and women's 4x400m relays, with Trinidad & Tobago and USA the respective defending champions. There are also finals in the field in the women's pole vault and men's triple jump. With analysis from Michael Johnson(n)

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Investigates
programme.desc: The presenter meets a family of arms dealers in Arkansas who sell high-grade weapons to more than 100 countries. She discovers how deals are made in this secretive business, which arms military forces, law enforcement agencies, private security companies and private individuals, and learns about the code of ethics governing the trade. She pays a visit to a gun factory, gets to fire one of the world's most powerful sniper rifles, and examines some of the latest technological breakthroughs, such as guns with facial recognition software(n)

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Louise and Tom decide they are too easily distracted by arguments that go nowhere, and try to adopt a new outlook. Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike(n)

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Tom and Louise look back on their early love life, and try to decide if they can see a future together. Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and a cast of comedy correspondents including Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on the week's news(n)

programme.title: Love in the Countryside
programme.desc: Katy picks her four favourite letter writers to meet face-to-face at a speed dating event in London, including 74-year-old former professional footballer Carl, and painter and decorator John. Meanwhile, it's decision time for dairy farmers Martin and Grace, who have each invited three potential suitors back to their farms. Both are looking for a partner that will fall in love with not only them, but also their rural lifestyle and are hoping sparks will fly with at least one of their guests during their weekend stay(n)

programme.title: The Big Hospital Experiment
programme.desc: In a radical social experiment based on a scheme in Germany, 14 young volunteers between the ages of 18 and 24 spend four weeks delivering patient care on the NHS frontline at Royal Derby Hospital, aiming to find out whether volunteers could help relieve the pressures of staff on the wards and make a positive difference to patient care in the NHS. As the volunteers embark on their final week of the scheme, The Royal Derby Hospital senior staff assess the success of the experiment. Meanwhile, Michael struggles to perform personal care for patients in the elderly care ward, and the volunteers' personal prejudices are challenged when a homeless patient who is suffering from alcoholism is brought into the hospital(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents topical debate from the National Assembly for Wales in Cardiff. The panellists are Conservative Party Chairman James Cleverly, Shadow Solicitor General Nick Thomas-Symonds, Plaid Cymru leader Adam Price, Leader of the Brexit Party in the Welsh Assembly Mark Reckless, and businesswoman Gina Miller, one of the complainants against the Government's decision to prorogue Parliament(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Evan shows no sign of giving up and pursues Chloe to a cottage in the countryside, where his obsession with her takes a violent turn that leaves her in fear for her life. Cameron receives bad news that forces him to abandon his efforts to help Ric, who has finally been forced to acknowledge his deteriorating health(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine enjoys the fun of the fair, while Angus Ashworth heads to Plymouth to discover the turbulent history of gin, before having a go as a Master Distiller. They then join up at their final auction in Clevedon, north Somerset(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich are tasked to create a garden that fuses Japan and Yorkshire - in Cheshunt, Hertfordshire. Yorkshire-born James wants to see hints of his native county in his new Home Counties house, while his Japanese wife Akika, who he met when traveling, naturally wants to feel a link to her upbringing and homeland(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A lifeboat crew in Dorset faces the challenge of getting urgent medical aid to a rock climber, who has fallen more than 40 feet and landed on an inaccessible rocky ledge. In Cornwall, a paraglider misjudges his launch and ends up crashing into the sea, while the Redcar crew search for a man who has drifted over a mile offshore in a small toy dinghy(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn presents live coverage of the Conservative Party autumn conference at the Manchester Central Convention Complex(n)

programme.title: Canoeing
programme.desc: Matthew Pinsent presents action from the opening meeting of the season, held at Lee Valley White Water Centre in London. With analysis from Etienne Stott(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of day four of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Great Britain's Dina Asher-Smith begins her bid for the 200m title in the heats, and the women's 400m also gets under way, while the women's javelin qualifying takes place in the field(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents further coverage of day four of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Norwegian star Karsten Warholm will be looking to defend his 400m hurdles title, but is expected to face competition from home favourite Abderrahman Samba and America's Rai Benjamin. Elsewhere, Great Britain's Lynsey Sharp will hope to be in contention in the 800m, and finals also take place in the women's high jump and 3,000m steeplechase, and the men's 5,000m and discus(n)

programme.title: What Britain Buys and Sells in a Day
programme.desc: As the nation's trading relationships face their biggest change in decades, Ed Balls, Ade Adepitan and Cherry Healey explore the science and systems that enable Britain to import and export goods around the world on an unprecedented scale. Ed is at the London Gateway port to reveal all the tricks of the fruit and vegetable trade, Ade visits Peru to see how Britain's obsession with healthy eating has created a booming economy for avocado growers, and Cherry meets a British farmer keeping the Middle East supplied with apples(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Eurovision winner Dana International introduces a look back at a year in which Lock, Stock and Two Smoking Barrels shot director Guy Ritchie to fame, There's Something About Mary had cinema audiences roaring with laughter and adult animation South Park took the world by storm. Goodness Gracious Me received a warm reception, the campaign to free Coronation Street's Deirdre Rachid began and George Michael found himself in a compromising situation(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Today at Conference
programme.desc: A review of the day's events at the Conservative party conference at the Manchester Central Convention Complex(n)

programme.title: The Cult Next Door
programme.desc: The extraordinary story of a strange cult that came to light in 2013, when three women emerged from a small flat in Brixton, south London, after decades in captivity. The documentary traces the group back to its roots in the 1970s, when Aravindan Balakrishnan, who believed in an international communist revolution, created a tiny political sect that followed the teachings of China's Chairman Mao. However, his left-wing collective evolved into a pseudo-religious cult, in which members were controlled, threatened and brainwashed so that they were too terrified to leave(n)

programme.title: Britain's Secret Seas
programme.desc: Paul Rose, Tooni Mahto and Frank Pope assist with the excavation of a 17th-century ship discovered in the waters outside Poole Harbour, Dorset, which could be one of the most important finds in British maritime history. They also learn about the plight of spiny seahorses in Dorset's Studland Bay, explore marine life beneath Brighton Pier and examine the wreck of an experimental Second World War submarine(n)

programme.title: Life in the Air
programme.desc: The final episode of the series features footage shot from within a giant flock of birds, revealing how half a million of them can fly in a group without colliding with each other. The programme also showcases the world's fastest airborne courtship display, a bird that savagely defends its territory, and conflict between moths and bats in the South American jungle(n)

programme.title: Julieta (2016)
programme.desc: A chance encounter with a childhood friend of her estranged daughter causes a guilt-ridden Madrid woman to rethink her future. Pedro Almodovar's drama, starring Emma Suarez and Adriana Ugarte. In Spanish(n)

programme.title: Detectorists
programme.desc: The return of Mackenzie Crook's award-winning comedy in which he and Toby Jones star as metal detector enthusiasts in search of their hearts' desire. Andy is considering internet dating. Not for him, but for Lance, who is still not over the departure of his ex-wife. Meanwhile, a mysterious stranger arrives to tempt the metal detecting club - in more ways than one. Real-life mother and daughter Diana Rigg and Rachael Stirling co-star, with Gerard Horan and Sophie Thompson(n)

programme.title: Detectorists
programme.desc: Sophie seems to be making progress with her new friend at the library, while the club is visited by the mayor on a matter of some delicacy. Peter's quest to find his grandmother has attracted the attention of some old enemies and Andy spots something suspicious. Comedy, starring Mackenzie Crook, Toby Jones, Aimee-Ffion Edwards and Daniel Donskov(n)

programme.title: Detectorists
programme.desc: Russell and Hugh embark on their mission to recover the mayor's chain of office from the car park in Barnfather Woods. Sophie proves she's still very bad at spying, while Becky and Lance each have something important to tell Andy. Comedy, starring Mackenzie Crook, Toby Jones, Aimee-Ffion Edwards and Rachael Stirling(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Peter Powell present the edition that first aired on June 30, featuring Hazell Dean, Bruce Springsteen, Tracy Chapman, Matt Bianco, Salt-N-Pepa, T'Pau, the Fat Boys and Chubby Checker, Bros and Glenn Medeiros(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Janice Long and Simon Mayo introduce the edition first aired on July 7, featuring performances by the Communards, the Mac Band featuring the McCampbell Brothers, Everything but the Girl, INXS, Eighth Wonder, Glenn Medeiros and Transvision Vamp(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Laure returns early from maternity leave to help the team put a name to an anonymous murder victim identifiable only by use of his dismembered torso. Meanwhile, a high-profile criminal trial tempts a jaded Joséphine, and magistrate Roban grows distracted. French crime drama, starring Caroline Proust(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Laure and Gilou reunite with one of their former colleagues while investigating their latest victim, and lawyer Joséphine pushes her client to plead guilty. In French(n)

programme.title: Britain's Secret Seas
programme.desc: Paul Rose, Tooni Mahto and Frank Pope assist with the excavation of a 17th-century ship discovered in the waters outside Poole Harbour, Dorset, which could be one of the most important finds in British maritime history. They also learn about the plight of spiny seahorses in Dorset's Studland Bay, explore marine life beneath Brighton Pier and examine the wreck of an experimental Second World War submarine(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Kelly Somers presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal Women v Brighton & Hove Albion Women at Meadow Lane, Manchester United Women v Liverpool Ladies at Leigh Sports Village, and West Ham United Ladies v Tottenham Hotspur Women at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: South Downs: England's Mountains Green
programme.desc: Peter Owen-Jones takes viewers into heart of the UK's newest National Park - the South Downs. Following the South Downs Way along the spine of the park, from the Seven Sisters Cliffs to Winchester - the ancient capital of England - Peter experiences a year exploring the park's landscapes, rich history, wildlife and people(n)

programme.title: Upstream
programme.desc: Over three years, cameras follow the course of the river Dee in Scotland to its source in the Cairngorm mountains, the highest of any river in Britain. The film, by writer Robert Macfarlane and director Rob Petit, begins in the lowland floodplains of the Dee, before travelling up, against the flow of the river, into Britain's own Arctic wilderness(n)

programme.title: Rhyme and Reason: BBC Introducing Arts
programme.desc: A compilation of short films, offering a snapshot of a new generation of artists who have been inspired by poetry and the spoken word(n)

programme.title: Lost Films of WW2
programme.desc: Part one of two. Rarely-seen home movies are shown together for the first time to retell the story of Britain at war from a uniquely vivid and personal perspective. Cine-reel footage is interwoven with testimony from members of the filmmakers' families and those who lived through the war, to give a first-hand account of life during the conflict. From footage shot by a Middlesbrough dentist who witnessed the rise of Nazism on a family holiday to the continent in 1939, to images of the evacuation of Dunkirk secretly recorded by a young naval officer, the films show momentous events through the eyes of the ordinary people caught up in them(n)

programme.title: Lost Films of WW2
programme.desc: Part two of two. Footage shot by an officer on HMS Kent during the Battle for the Mediterranean, and the D-Day landings filmed by a naval lieutenant on his personal camera(n)

programme.title: The Sinner
programme.desc: During an afternoon at the beach with her husband and son, a woman is consumed by a fit of rage and commits a startling and very public act of violence without understanding why, but Detective Harry Ambrose is determined to find the answer. Crime drama, starring Jessica Biel and Bill Pullman(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Josephine is left shaken after an assault, Roban uses unconventional methods to build his case, and Gilou and Laure question two brothers who claim to have gone straight. In French(n)

programme.title: Spiral

programme.title: South Downs: England's Mountains Green
programme.desc: Peter Owen-Jones takes viewers into heart of the UK's newest National Park - the South Downs. Following the South Downs Way along the spine of the park, from the Seven Sisters Cliffs to Winchester - the ancient capital of England - Peter experiences a year exploring the park's landscapes, rich history, wildlife and people(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days

programme.title: James May: The Reassembler

programme.title: Cake Bakers and Trouble Makers
programme.desc: In a programme originally shown to mark the Women's Institute's centenary year, historian Lucy Worsley goes beyond the stereotypes of jam and Jerusalem to reveal the surprisingly radical side of this British institution. She reveals that the WI's humble origins in an Anglesey garden shed in 1915 were no bar to the movement's grand ambitions to be an organisation engaged in the fight for women's rights. She explores some of the WI's most important campaigns, including its 1918 crusade for decent housing and its remarkably radical fight for equal pay in 1943(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: On June 4, 1989 ,the world's biggest, longest, and most famous pro-democracy demonstration was brought to a tragic end in Tiananmen Square. The images from those final bloody days remain potent and the death toll is still disputed. This documentary combines eyewitness accounts and leaked secret documents to provide a deeper understanding of how the events of those seven weeks unfolded in Beijing and behind the scenes, changing the course of Chinese history.(n)

programme.title: I Was There: Kate Adie on Tiananmen Square
programme.desc: Kate Adie re-examines her historic coverage of the massacre in Tiananmen Square, Beijing, in June 1989, revealing how she was wounded by gunfire and narrowly escaped death herself as an estimated 2,000 pro-democracy demonstrators were shot by Chinese government troops. Drawing on the BBC film archive, she also assesses how China has been portrayed before and after this period of upheaval, with additional insight from historian Steve Tsang(n)

programme.title: The Story of China
programme.desc: Michael Wood tells the story of the world's newest superpower, exploring the stories, people, culture and landscapes that have helped create its character over 4,000 years. He begins by looking at ancestry, one of the great themes of Chinese history. He joins a 300-strong family as they reunite to carry out rituals for an ancestor who died in 1049, witnesses a million pilgrims at the shrine of the goddess Nu Wa, and hears about China's greatest archaeological discovery, the Oracle Bones, which reveal fascinating clues about the first great dynasty - the Shang(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to rural Gloucestershire to help a couple of empty nesters search for a home with a £700,000 budget. While in the area, he also visits the Cotswold Gliding Club(n)

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway heads to the hilltop Town of Giants on the Llyn Peninsula and then explores the island of Flat Holm - the most southerly point of Wales. He also visits the ruins of Britain's only offshore cholera hospital(n)

programme.title: Iolo's Street Life
programme.desc: Iolo Williams finds birds and bees are active on the streets of Colwyn Bay. Robins, sparrows, tits, wagtails and pigeons are nesting in drain pipes, road signs, wall holes, security cages and, in Brecon, even on a traffic light. In the south Wales valleys' town of Deri, what can only be described as a nature reserve has been created in the back garden of a terraced house, attracting numerous hedgehogs to feast(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present an edition of the quiz featuring people famed for their voices, with contestants India Fisher, Nicholas Briggs, Kate Robbins, Steve Nallon, Redd Pepper, Richard Ridings, Felicity Finch and Tim Bentinck(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Joe Crowley is in Cumbria to explore the Lake District's wild side. meeting a man who runs pony treks that follow old pack-horse routes across the fells. Joe then hears more about the region's industrial heritage as he meets artists behind a new installation celebrating the area's copper mining history. Plus, Charlotte Smith investigating the rise of far-right extremism in the countryside(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from the first two live weekends. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off to decide who will be the first to leave the contest. With music by Lewis Capaldi(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Lytham Hall in Lancashire, where items brought in include a rare Victorian automaton, a work by LS Lowry and medals won by Britain's first black Olympian. John Foster marvels at a briefcase full of artefacts from Ernest Shackleton's 1909 Antarctic expedition and Ronnie Morgan discovers a gift from a Russian tsar(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: Drama following people from all walks of life during the first year of the Second World War, beginning in the run-up to its outbreak. American journalist and broadcaster Nancy Campbell makes a grisly discovery at the Polish-German border, while in Manchester, Lois waits for news of her translator boyfriend Harry. However, Lois is unaware that Harry has a secret Polish lover, Kasia, and he has vowed to help her flee from Warsaw. Starring Sean Bean, Helen Hunt, Lesley Manville, Julia Brown and Jonah Hauer-King(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Leicester City v Newcastle United. Mark Chapman presents action from the only Premier League fixture of the day, held at King Power Stadium. The sides have already met in the second round of the Carabao Cup this term, with Leicester prevailing on penalties at St James' Park after the match ended 1-1. Plus, a round up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Kelly Somers presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal Women v Brighton & Hove Albion Women at Meadow Lane, Manchester United Women v Liverpool Ladies at Leigh Sports Village, and West Ham United Ladies v Tottenham Hotspur Women at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: A World Athletics Championships edition of the quiz, which sees former Great Britain stars Denise Lewis, Liz McColgan, Greg Rutherford and Kriss Akabusi join host Sue Barker and team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson on the quiz(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets Oscar-winning actress Helen Mirren, who is starring in the new TV drama Catherine the Great. Author, adventurer and documentary-maker Simon Reeve also appears, as does comedian and actor Jack Whitehall, who stood in as host for Graham during the spring. Plus, the one and only Rupaul discusses bringing his hit show to the BBC in RuPaul's Drag Race UK. Music is supplied courtesy of Danish popsters Alphabeat(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Consumer affairs show tackling issues around the UK. In a battle against suspect deals, the team digs into the facts and demands answers from people at the top, as well as showing viewers how to get the necessary information to make practical choices about their finances(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin explore a two-bed terrace in Salford, a three-bed semi in Derbyshire and a one-bedroom flat in south London(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: Following the investigation by an elite team of Scotland Yard detectives into the biggest organised crime gangs in London, which uncovered the drugs kingpin behind a multimillion-pound racket. By making his own drugs delivery, Paul Monk blew his cover and, when his associates were arrested, fled abroad(n)

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Rob and Gerald brave the red carpet at a celebrity hotspot in London to try to recover the unpaid wages of a former worker, and there is an angry confrontation in a motorcycle shop when Mike and Lawrence try to remove bikes to collect a debt owed to a customer(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and Nick Hall help two teams look for items in the West Sussex town of Ardingly, while presenter Anita Manning investigates the history of funfairs(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Eve is a little more optimistic about the future and Valerie faces a tragedy, while Rob sustains an injury at work, and one of his colleagues acts strangely around the hospital(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in rural Somerset with two sport enthusiasts who have a £1million budget and want to buy a country home with views and a garden for football(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell go in search for items in Cumbria, setting out from Cockermouth and heading towards an auction near Kendal(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal is joined by three of the couples, including the first to be eliminated from the competition after losing the dance-off(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Matt Baker and Michelle Ackerley present(n)

programme.title: Down the Social with Owen Money
programme.desc: The comedian and Mandy Starr head to Neath Working Men's Social Club to discover the pulling power of tribute acts inspired by stars from Tina Turner to Tom Jones. They meet two sisters who have been coming to the club weekly for half a century, and Owen has a lesson in bingo etiquette(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben agrees to go on a date with Callum, although he begins to wonder if it is such a good idea after both Ian and Jay comment on his history with men - and things only get worse when Stuart pleads with him not to hurt his brother. Bex is fed up of Sonia fussing over university so by way of distraction she organises a baby shower for Louise. Mel continues to pressurise Sharon into getting her a villa in Portugal, Denise confronts Wanda over her treatment of Ted and Jean supports Daniel at his hospital appointments(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Investigates
programme.desc: Wyre Davies is on the trail of the people behind the multimillion-pound puppy industry to find out what is really going on inside licensed premises. In this BBC Wales Investigates programme, he confronts the breeders, inspectors and vets who should be policing the trade(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The eight remaining contestants take part in the first of the semi-finals and face their toughest challenge yet as they battle gale force winds as they cook in tents perched high on top of the White Cliffs of Dover. They need to defy the elements to serve up a feast for 120 HM Coastguard staff and volunteers responsible for keeping one of the world's busiest shipping lanes safe. They then head back to the MasterChef kitchen for an invention test, before two more celebrities are sent home(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A year on from the assassination of Jamal Khashoggi, Jane Corbin investigates the brutal murder of the journalist inside the Saudi consulate in Istanbul. She speaks to some of the very few people who have listened to covert recordings from inside the diplomatic building, revealing the full details of what happened to Khashoggi, the hit squad sent to kill him and the cover-up that followed. She also hears evidence that responsibility for the murder goes to the highest levels of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and asks whether the West has turned a blind eye to state-sanctioned murder so that lucrative trade deals can flourish(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a documentary following young people from two local estates in London's Waltham Forest as they take part in a ground-breaking arts intervention programme designed to change the course of their lives for good. With help from mentors including Young Vic artistic director Kwame Kwei-Armah, EastEnders star Tameka Empson and composer Talvin Singh, the cast write and perform a musical based on their lives, as part of the capital's first Borough of Culture celebrations(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ching-He Huang and Cyrus Todiwala, and special guest Alexander Armstrong. Plus, archive clips featuring Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, and Nigella Lawson(n)

programme.title: Mary Berry's Quick Cooking
programme.desc: Mary heads into London's West End for inspiration, seeking out the secrets of getting busy people fed quickly and well. She learns the art of high-speed wok cooking in Chinatown and sampling the sweet treats being made daily at London's first-ever patisserie, before seeing a show and having a late-night cocktail with some of the cast members. Back in her own kitchen, Mary creates an Asian beef and pepper stir fry, a chocolate cappuccino pie and an easy, speedy breakfast for those on the go(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Everton v Manchester City. Plus, updates on the lunchtime game between Sheffield United and Liverpool(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a bungalow in Cheshire and an old chapel in Dudley ripe for development into flats. Martel Maxwell is in West Yorkshire, where the new owners of an end of terrace have grand plans for a child-friendly development(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of day two of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Great Britain's Dina Asher-Smith begins her bid for a sprint double with the heats of the 100m, while other events include the heats of the men's 800m and qualifying for the men's discus and pole vault. With analysis from Michael Johnson(n)

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie presents the classified results and a round-up of the afternoon's main football news. Plus, a look back at the week's talking points(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. As always, the challengers are tasked with coming up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. This time, all the contestants come from the world of music, as Tom Ogden, Ray BLK, Shaun Ryder, Rowetta, Ricky Wilson, Will Young, Naughty Boy and Lesley Garrett take part. Plus, a special performance from the band Blossoms(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the first live episode of this year's series, with a line-up featuring the likes of Alex Scott, Emma Barton and James Cracknell. The celebrities are dancing to impress the four judges and the viewers at home, who get the chance to vote for their favourites for the very first time this series(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Hospital drama with the staff of Holby's accident and emergency department as they deal with more sick and injured patients. David and Ollie continue to clash after Ollie's operation, and David is taken aback when he finds out that Ollie has invited Rosa round to keep him company. Meanwhile, Rash decides to fight to stay in Holby, and new paramedic Lev arrives late for work after saving some kittens. Starring Jason Durr, Harry Collett, Jacey Salles, Neet Mohan and Uriel Emil(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Cathy seems reluctant to reveal any information about her new boyfriend, Mark refuses to talk about why his wife has thrown him out, and Dermot lies to his fiancee's parents about his job - all of which makes Agnes feel her family is becoming too secretive. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton travels through the Borders, learning handling techniques at a school for sheepdogs and then heading to some man-made lochs in the Moorfoot Hills. In Edinburgh, he goes in search of a lost river and two long-vanished lochs in the heart of the city, before walking in the footsteps of Mary, Queen of Scots on a allegedly haunted island in Loch Leven(n)

programme.desc: Ben agrees to go on a date with Callum, although he begins to wonder if it is such a good idea after both Ian and Jay comment on his history with men - and things only get worse when Stuart pleads with him not to hurt his brother. programme.title: The Cameron Years
programme.desc: The second of the two-part documentary begins in 2005 at a moment of crisis for the Conservative Party, following three successive general election defeats. David Cameron, a relatively new MP, and his small group of supporters sought to radically shift the party's direction, taking on veteran opponent David Davis in a leadership election. Here, Cameron re-examines his desire to reshape the party - and how he risked alienating the powerful old guard as he set out to prioritise issues such as environmentalism, social justice and international aid(n)

Back in her own kitchen, Mary creates an Asian beef and pepper stir fry, a chocolate cappuccino pie and an easy, speedy breakfast for those on the go(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Everton v Manchester City. Plus, updates on the lunchtime game between Sheffield United and Liverpool(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a bungalow in Cheshire and an old chapel in Dudley ripe for development into flats. Martel Maxwell is in West Yorkshire, where the new owners of an end of terrace have grand plans for a child-friendly development(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of day two of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Great Britain's Dina Asher-Smith begins her bid for a sprint double with the heats of the 100m, while other events include the heats of the men's 800m and qualifying for the men's discus and pole vault. With analysis from Michael Johnson(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Final Score from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's action along with the classified results(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. As always, the challengers are tasked with coming up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. This time, all the contestants come from the world of music, as Tom Ogden, Ray BLK, Shaun Ryder, Rowetta, Ricky Wilson, Will Young, Naughty Boy and Lesley Garrett take part. Plus, a special performance from the band Blossoms(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the first live episode of this year's series, with a line-up featuring the likes of Alex Scott, Emma Barton and James Cracknell. The celebrities are dancing to impress the four judges and the viewers at home, who get the chance to vote for their favourites for the very first time this series(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Hospital drama with the staff of Holby's accident and emergency department as they deal with more sick and injured patients. David and Ollie continue to clash after Ollie's operation, and David is taken aback when he finds out that Ollie has invited Rosa round to keep him company. Meanwhile, Rash decides to fight to stay in Holby, and new paramedic Lev arrives late for work after saving some kittens. Starring Jason Durr, Harry Collett, Jacey Salles, Neet Mohan and Uriel Emil(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Cathy seems reluctant to reveal any information about her new boyfriend, Mark refuses to talk about why his wife has thrown him out, and Dermot lies to his fiancee's parents about his job - all of which makes Agnes feel her family is becoming too secretive. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Sheffield United v Liverpool at Bramall Lane and Everton v Manchester City at Goodison Park, as both of last season's top two faced potentially difficult away games. Plus, Aston Villa v Burnley, Bournemouth v West Ham United, Chelsea v Brighton & Hove Albion, Crystal Palace v Norwich City, Tottenham Hotspur v Southampton and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Watford(n)

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: For their final challenge, the remaining artists are given advice on how to survive fame courtesy of Stefflon Don, before performing on the Kenny Allstar show(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game. Plus, a look at the major news stories shaping the American football world, as the leading sides in the NFL continue their bids to succeed Philadelphia Eagles as Super Bowl-winners(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Babita Sharma present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Sheffield United v Liverpool at Bramall Lane and Everton v Manchester City at Goodison Park, as both of last season's top two faced potentially difficult away games. Plus, Aston Villa v Burnley, Bournemouth v West Ham United, Chelsea v Brighton & Hove Albion, Crystal Palace v Norwich City, Tottenham Hotspur v Southampton and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Watford(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around the Province. With Mark Carruthers(n)

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Ria Hebden host topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Ramoths head to Perth for a trial week, where Lauren has her work cut out in persuading husband Lee that a move to Australia is the right thing for them and their children(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion challenges two teams to scour an antiques fair in Dorking, Surrey, to find items to sell at auction, with Caroline Hawley and David Harper offering advice(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones is at Leicester's De Montfort Hall to count down the UK's 10 favourite hymns as voted for by viewers, with performances of the chosen tunes by the Kingdom Choir, Lesley Garrett, Colin Thackery, Russell Watson, Sope Aluko, Eddi Reader, Katherine Jenkins and Daniel O' Donnell(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Tina Daheley introduces viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the progress of properties sold at auction in Shropshire, Derby and Cumbria(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to rural Gloucestershire to help a couple of empty nesters search for a home with a £700,000 budget. While in the area, he also visits the Cotswold Gliding Club(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and Jay Blades transform items reclaimed from a tip, before giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of them in the first place(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present an edition of the quiz featuring people famed for their voices, with contestants India Fisher, Nicholas Briggs, Kate Robbins, Steve Nallon, Redd Pepper, Richard Ridings, Felicity Finch and Tim Bentinck(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Joe Crowley is in Cumbria to explore the Lake District's wild side. meeting a man who runs pony treks that follow old pack-horse routes across the fells. Joe then hears more about the region's industrial heritage as he meets artists behind a new installation celebrating the area's copper mining history. Plus, Charlotte Smith investigating the rise of far-right extremism in the countryside(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from the first two live weekends. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off to decide who will be the first to leave the contest. With music by Lewis Capaldi(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Lytham Hall in Lancashire, where items brought in include a rare Victorian automaton, a work by LS Lowry and medals won by Britain's first black Olympian. John Foster marvels at a briefcase full of artefacts from Ernest Shackleton's 1909 Antarctic expedition and Ronnie Morgan discovers a gift from a Russian tsar(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: Drama following people from all walks of life during the first year of the Second World War, beginning in the run-up to its outbreak. American journalist and broadcaster Nancy Campbell makes a grisly discovery at the Polish-German border, while in Manchester, Lois waits for news of her translator boyfriend Harry. However, Lois is unaware that Harry has a secret Polish lover, Kasia, and he has vowed to help her flee from Warsaw. Starring Sean Bean, Helen Hunt, Lesley Manville, Julia Brown and Jonah Hauer-King(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Leicester City v Newcastle United. Mark Chapman presents action from the only Premier League fixture of the day, held at King Power Stadium. The sides have already met in the second round of the Carabao Cup this term, with Leicester prevailing on penalties at St James' Park after the match ended 1-1. Plus, a round up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Kelly Somers presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal Women v Brighton & Hove Albion Women at Meadow Lane, Manchester United Women v Liverpool Ladies at Leigh Sports Village, and West Ham United Ladies v Tottenham Hotspur Women at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: A World Athletics Championships edition of the quiz, which sees former Great Britain stars Denise Lewis, Liz McColgan, Greg Rutherford and Kriss Akabusi join host Sue Barker and team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson on the quiz(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets Oscar-winning actress Helen Mirren, who is starring in the new TV drama Catherine the Great. Author, adventurer and documentary-maker Simon Reeve also appears, as does comedian and actor Jack Whitehall, who stood in as host for Graham during the spring. Plus, the one and only Rupaul discusses bringing his hit show to the BBC in RuPaul's Drag Race UK. Music is supplied courtesy of Danish popsters Alphabeat(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Consumer affairs show tackling issues around the UK. In a battle against suspect deals, the team digs into the facts and demands answers from people at the top, as well as showing viewers how to get the necessary information to make practical choices about their finances(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin explore a two-bed terrace in Salford, a three-bed semi in Derbyshire and a one-bedroom flat in south London(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: Following the investigation by an elite team of Scotland Yard detectives into the biggest organised crime gangs in London, which uncovered the drugs kingpin behind a multimillion-pound racket. By making his own drugs delivery, Paul Monk blew his cover and, when his associates were arrested, fled abroad(n)

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Rob and Gerald brave the red carpet at a celebrity hotspot in London to try to recover the unpaid wages of a former worker, and there is an angry confrontation in a motorcycle shop when Mike and Lawrence try to remove bikes to collect a debt owed to a customer(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and Nick Hall help two teams look for items in the West Sussex town of Ardingly, while presenter Anita Manning investigates the history of funfairs(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Eve is a little more optimistic about the future and Valerie faces a tragedy, while Rob sustains an injury at work, and one of his colleagues acts strangely around the hospital(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in rural Somerset with two sport enthusiasts who have a £1million budget and want to buy a country home with views and a garden for football(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell go in search for items in Cumbria, setting out from Cockermouth and heading towards an auction near Kendal(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal is joined by three of the couples, including the first to be eliminated from the competition after losing the dance-off(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Matt Baker and Michelle Ackerley present(n)

programme.title: Parents' Evening
programme.desc: The return of the documentary examining the meetings between parents and teachers, beginning with one for Year 14 at Oakgrove Integrated College in Londonderry. The programme finds out how Jack, Aliah and Aidan are coping with their final year of A-levels and if their grades will be good enough to get into university(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben agrees to go on a date with Callum, although he begins to wonder if it is such a good idea after both Ian and Jay comment on his history with men - and things only get worse when Stuart pleads with him not to hurt his brother. Bex is fed up of Sonia fussing over university so by way of distraction she organises a baby shower for Louise. Mel continues to pressurise Sharon into getting her a villa in Portugal, Denise confronts Wanda over her treatment of Ted and Jean supports Daniel at his hospital appointments(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A year on from the assassination of Jamal Khashoggi, Jane Corbin investigates the brutal murder of the journalist inside the Saudi consulate in Istanbul. She speaks to some of the very few people who have listened to covert recordings from inside the diplomatic building, revealing the full details of what happened to Khashoggi, the hit squad sent to kill him and the cover-up that followed. She also hears evidence that responsibility for the murder goes to the highest levels of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and asks whether the West has turned a blind eye to state-sanctioned murder so that lucrative trade deals can flourish(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The eight remaining contestants take part in the first of the semi-finals and face their toughest challenge yet as they battle gale force winds as they cook in tents perched high on top of the White Cliffs of Dover. They need to defy the elements to serve up a feast for 120 HM Coastguard staff and volunteers responsible for keeping one of the world's busiest shipping lanes safe. They then head back to the MasterChef kitchen for an invention test, before two more celebrities are sent home(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: The story of a man who abandoned a financial career to pursue a childhood ambition to become a professional rabbit-catcher. He rejects modern pest control methods such as gas or poisons as environmentally dangerous, instead favouring the traditional skills he learned during his childhood in Magilligan, which involve using ferrets and dogs to herd animals towards him, so he can humanely kill them(n)

programme.title: Pop Goes Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Music and news from 1975, the year in which the IRA declared an indefinite ceasefire and secret talks took place between the British government and Sinn Fein. William Craig presented a compromise of voluntary power-sharing that was hoped would form a solution to Northern Ireland's divisions - but it was swiftly rejected by Unionists(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a documentary following young people from two local estates in London's Waltham Forest as they take part in a ground-breaking arts intervention programme designed to change the course of their lives for good. With help from mentors including Young Vic artistic director Kwame Kwei-Armah, EastEnders star Tameka Empson and composer Talvin Singh, the cast write and perform a musical based on their lives, as part of the capital's first Borough of Culture celebrations(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: Argentina v Tonga (Kick-off 5.45am). Coverage of the Pool C clash, which takes place at Hanazono Rugby Stadium in Higashiosaka City, Japan. Argentina have a commendable World Cup pedigree having reached the last four in 2007 and 2015, and despite a poor run of form this year will be considered favourites for victory here. Presented by Mark Pougatch, with analysis from Brian O'Driscoll, Michael Lynagh and Paul O'Connell, and commentary by Simon Ward and Danielle Waterman(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: Japan v Ireland (Kick-off 8.15am). Mark Pougatch presents all the action from the Pool A encounter at Tokyo Stadium. Ireland entered the tournament as favourites to win the pool, but they will not be taking the hosts lightly after they shocked the rugby world four years ago with a victory over South Africa. With analysis from Brian O'Driscoll, Sam Warburton and Paul O'Connell, reports by Jill Douglas, and commentary from Miles Harrison, Alan Quinlan and Shane Williams(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: South Africa v Namibia (Kick-off 10.45am). Craig Doyle presents coverage of the Pool B match at City of Toyota Stadium in Japan. The Springboks start as strong favourites against their African neighbours, but will be wary of complacency after their shock defeat at the hands of Japan four years ago in Brighton. With analysis from Maggie Alphonsi, Sam Warburton and Lawrence Dallaglio, reports by Jill Douglas and Bryan Habana, and commentary from Nick Mullins, Ben Kay and Ugo Monye(n)

programme.title: Casper (1995)
programme.desc: A paranormal expert moves into a haunted house where his daughter befriends the amiable ghost of a young boy - but things start to go awry when three smelly spooks and two dastardly crooks try to chase the new residents away. Family comedy, starring Christina Ricci, Bill Pullman, Eric Idle, Cathy Moriarty and featuring the voice of Malachi Pearson(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking contestants Sophie, Richard and Linda to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. A seat becomes vacant on the local council when Sam Popham is taken ill, so Pop stands in the election, and Mariette returns from holiday with Charley. David Jason stars(n)

programme.desc: Part two of two. Pop and Ma become the target of dirty tricks and smear tactics during the election campaign, and Charley is placed in a compromising position by Abigail's teacher(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper sleuth returns to his home country of Belgium for the first time since the First World War to attend a medal ceremony, only to find a murder case needs solving. Agatha Christie mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Chancellor and David De Keyser(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The peace of the sleepy hamlet of Little Worthy is disturbed when a man's body is found tied down in a miniature village - just like the hero in classic tale Gulliver's Travels. As the investigation begins, Barnaby has no shortage of suspects, including the victim's recently jilted partner - a teacher whose classroom is full of paintings of the story - his teenage lover, a caretaker and a hotel owner. But the case takes a twist when the killer strikes again amid the chaos of the annual fancy-dress boat race. Detective drama, guest starring Jesse Birdsall and Olivia Colman, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A burglar dubbed 'The Creeper' targets the villages of Midsomer as Barnaby and Jones investigate the murder of a guest at an upper-crust dinner party. As the case progresses, Barnaby becomes the thief's latest victim and it emerges that several high-society friends with connections to gangsters and a police chief have a few secrets worth killing for. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Jason Hughes, with guest appearances from Rik Mayall and Jenny Agutter(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Schoolteacher Connie Bishop keeps waking in the night, convinced she has an intruder, but most of the villagers in Badger's Drift dismiss her fears as an overactive imagination. Days later, a local councillor thought to have been obsessed with her is found murdered in her garden, and the ensuing investigation by Barnaby and Jones uncovers jealous rivals, ruthless social climbers and a cottage with a few secrets. Guest starring Paul Kaye, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When a man's charred remains are found after an explosion, Foyle's investigations lead him to a top secret intelligence organisation. With murder and deception rife among its members, the race is on to find out the truth, but the detective's discoveries threaten his future career. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen with guests Angela Thorne, Ronald Pickup and Samuel West(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A planning dispute leads to murder after a stalwart in the Midsomer Conservation Society suspects her neighbours are involved in a money-making development scam. Political wrangling, burglaries and sexual liaisons abound in picturesque Great Pelfe as Barnaby and Jones investigate. Guest starring Hugo Speer (Bleak House), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Oxford plays host to a veteran film star, in town to visit a local cinema. However, a mysterious death casts a sinister pall over the occasion, and offers the newly promoted DS Endeavour Morse a fresh case to pursue. Meanwhile, a family visit puts DCI Thursday's hospitality to the test. Inspector Morse prequel, starring Shaun Evans and Roger Allam(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A woman's body is pulled from the river, reminding Frost that he investigated the death of her husband's first wife years ago. Unfortunately, it turns out that a key witness's statement went astray during the earlier case - which leads to Frost being suspended from duty. Discreetly continuing his enquiries, he is intrigued to learn the local priest recently heard a very disturbing confession. Starring David Jason, Eoin McCarthy, Anthony Calf, Kenneth Cope and Isla Blair(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nick is worried about leaving Alice while he is on holiday and tells Frank to stop taking things out on him and Kathy. Zoe tells Archie that she could not manage without him(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lorraine tells Carol that she doesn't want her at her party. Jack finds out the hole is due to a collapsed mine. Zoe can't wait to leave her dad behind(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)
